title: Mr. Coffee Automatic Coffee Grinder with 5 Presets, 12 Cup Capacity, Black – Ideal for Home Use and Espresso Lovers
description:
About this item
Hands-free Grinding: Automatic settings for efficient, stress-free use
Chamber Cleaning System: Twists to clean grounds from chamber walls
Versatile Uses: 5 grind settings to customize your grind - fine to coarse
Easy to Clean: Removable grinding chamber ensures mess-free cleaning
Brew Control: 4-12 cup selector to grind only what you need
Compact Storage: Features cord storage for convenient storing
Safety First: Lid-activated safety switch ensures grinding only when the lid is locked
STAINLESS STEEL BLADES: Grinding technology uses sharp stainless steel blades for quick results
reviews_5s:
Play Video
As a coffee enthusiast, I've used various grinders over the years, but this one stands out for its simplicity, efficiency, and ease of use.

The grinder's 5 presets make it incredibly easy to achieve the perfect grind consistency for any brewing method. Whether I'm making espresso, drip coffee, or French press, I can simply select the appropriate preset and let the grinder do the rest. This feature takes the guesswork out of grinding and ensures consistent results every time.

The 12-cup capacity is another standout feature of this grinder. It's perfect for brewing large batches of coffee for gatherings or for stocking up on ground coffee for the week ahead. The spacious grinding chamber accommodates enough beans to meet my caffeine needs without the hassle of constant refilling.

One of the things I love most about this grinder is how easy it is to clean. The removable grinding chamber and lid can be easily washed by hand or placed in the dishwasher for hassle-free cleaning. The sleek black exterior wipes clean effortlessly, maintaining its appearance with minimal effort.

Additionally, the compact design of the grinder makes it easy to store on my countertop or in a cabinet when not in use. It doesn't take up much space, yet it packs a punch in terms of performance and functionality.This is a great upgrade, i love the ease of setting the grind level, amount and hit start. I also love the bowl & lid release for easy cleaning. Great productBought this to replace and older Mr. Coffee grinder that lasted for years. Really like the one touch automatic grinding, but this new one is quite loud compared to the older one (which still works). Where I could carry on a conversation while using the old one, it's difficult to talk over this one. Probably not a deal breaker, but something to be aware of.This coffee bean grinder is easy to use. It operates in a clean manner, leaving all the ground coffee within the grinding chamber. It doesn't allow some of the grounds to escape the grinding chamber and cause a mess to the exterior of the grinding chamber and to the countertop. However, when this coffee grinder sometime in the future needs replacement I think I'll spring for a more expensive model. One with a bean hopper that feeds the beans into a grinder and deposits the ground beans into a lower chamber. Why, you ask? So as not to have to extract the grounds from around the cutting blade and to grind a greater volume of beans. I see that Mr. Coffee makes such a model and I might have enjoyed it even more than the model I did buy.I absolutely love this coffee grinder. I just needed something compact and affordable for my counter and this fit the bill. I love that all I have to do is set how fine I want the coffee grounds to be and press the button and it grinds it and then shuts off automatically. I don’t have to stand there holding the button the whole time. I can easily get my coffee pot cleaned and setup while the grinder does it’s thing. Very quick and convenient. It’s made out of plastic but feels durable. I have had zero issues with it thus far and I have had it for 6+ months.I love to grind my coffee beans each morning and I'm the only one drinking it, so this is PERFECT for what I need. It has grind options from coarse to medium to fine with options in-between so I can get the perfect size grind for my espresso or cold brew or pour over! It's nice to just have to press the button once and not hold onto it so that I don't over or under grind the beans. I do wish the cord was a bit longer, but it's not a big deal and I really love having this little guy in my coffee making arsenal!Everything it advertised. Quieter than my old one. Easy to clean with lots a of grind options.Love this grinder its automatic
Just set the grind
For how many cups
Press the button and starts grinding.I used it to grind whole coffee beans!Works well.
reviews_1s:
It did grind coffee well but have to hold lid turned shut or it would shut off; grinding chamber already broke after 2 moths at poorly designed cam lock area and now has a hole in it rendering it useless. chamber cleaning device is worthless and doesn't even work when you pull grinding chamber off unit and dump it into coffee maker which is exactly when you need it to work. I've gone back to using my old IDS77 model
which still works well but blades are worn so a few more seconds to grind. dump it and chambermaid cleans out well still. I wish I could buy a new IDS77 instead. way better deviceWhat initially sold me on this was the fact it has 5 grind settings — perfecto, since sometimes I like to make espresso using my Moka pot or machine and sometimes I’ll use a French press or make cold brew… all needing a specific grind. I have an older version of a Mr Coffee bean grinder but the plastic cleaning arms broke so I figured it was time for a new one…. while I did see my older model for purchase with strong reviews compared to this model, I still went with this one having high hopes. As soon as I unboxed it I was already questioning if I should’ve gone with the original… to answer my question, YES, yes I should have. First of all, the quality of this looks like some cheap toy. Second, the lid didn’t fully lock into place. The whole thing just seems very poorly made. I still rinsed it out and let it dry overnight to prepare for first use. Complete disappointment. I put it on the 4th setting just in front of “fine” for a grind that should have been perfect for my Moka pot… but no. It ended up having chunks of bean left and then I had to re-grind which defeats the purpose of me just pushing the button and letting it do its thing without me having to monitor the grind quality and still manually find a good grind. Anyway, it’s already back in the box and ready to be returned — I’m going to get the older original model like the one I already have and maybe I’ll save up for one of those even more expensive fancier ones in the future with multiple grind settings.The grinding is extremely loud, extremely. Chamber that contains the coffee is plastic and grounds stick to the bottom and sides. It needs to be stainless steel. Tapping the sides of the chamber really doesn't work to get the grinds out and being that it is plastic, it could crack The 'fine ground' setting is not fine, but instead it gives you a course ground, had to grind twice to get a finer grind. It may be cheaper, but if the grinder doesn't do what it is supposed to do, its a waste of money.This coffee maker, delivered on 10/10/2024, was used once each day then washed, dried and left on the counter. Today, 12/24/2024 when I filled the reservoir to the 4 cup level, it immediately started leaking water on to the counter. this coffee maker is now unusable.I used it to grind spices, etc where I add water to the grinding chamber. Water runs right through it. I'm assuming there is a crack I can't see. Update: I received a replacement and it does the same thing. Now I can see the drainage holes which allow the contents to get into the main cabinet. Previous model did not do this.The lid disengages by itself while grinding. I need to hold the lid to keep it engaged with the safety switch. Also the unit allows some coffee grinds to escape while grinding.DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY!!! WORST DESIGN FLAW EVER!!!! GRRRRINDER SEEMS TO WORK OK THOUGH THE TOP CHIPPED RIGHT AFTER THE WARRANTY CAUSING THE POTENTIAL FOR CUTTING YOUR FINGER WHEN YOU REMOVE THE TOP AFTER GRRRRINDING. IT IS IMPOSSIBLE TO GET THE GRRRROUND COFFEE INTO YOUR COFFEEMAKER. THE FRESH GRRRRINDS SPILL ALL OVER THE COUNTER **EVERY TIME** DURING TRANSFER, MAKING A MESS **EVERY TIME** I GRRRRIND COFFEE!!!! TOO MUCH COFFEE LEFT IN THE GRRRRINDER AFTER SEVERAL ATTEMPTS TO EMPTY. DO NOT BUY!!!! GRRRRRR!!!! (WRITTEN BY MY WIFE)ordered the product on a limited time deal and it doesn't work at all!!!! which appears the only option I have is to return it for a full refund and then buy another one which of course now it's $10 higher in price and there's no way to contact the manufacturer. Amazon has to eat the costs & so do II don't like to leave negative reviews. However, this product deserves fair warning to other consumers.

I purchased the first one in Nov and received a warranty replacement for it already. Both have the same issues...
1. After a few uses, the lid doesn't not secure all the way down. You have to stand there and hold it down to work. I loved the feature of being able to multi-task while grinding coffee. I'm a multi-tasker and found this quite annoying.
2. After a few more uses, the removable chamber is no longer removeable. I don't know why. I clean the darned thing after each use, so there is no build up of coffee grinds.
3. So freaking loud!
4. Grinds are not consistent. Some ground well and the rest in chunks.

In my opinion, this product is poorly designed and executed.

Pro: their customer service is quick to respond and sent me a replacement without any hassle.

This purchase was equivalent to throwing money down the toilet. I've ordered another brand tonight as this one just isn't doing it for me.The cleaning mechanism keeps the beans from circulating well which developed very inconsistent grind. Chunks of beans next to well grounded particles. Also have to press button 2 or three times to get bean chunks ground up. Also it is quite loud.
